sections.paragraph:
ἔϑνος, ους, τό [cp. ἔθος; ‘a number of people or animals forming a group’, then strictly of humans as a ‘people group’]

—1. ‘a group of people viewed as an entity’, people (group)

—a. generically Mt 24:7, 14; Ac 17:26; Ro 1:5.

—b. specifically: of geographically determined people τὸ ἔθνος τῆς Σαμαρείας the Samaritan people Ac 8:9; of people determined by custom and tradition τὸ ἔθνος τῶν Ἰουδαίων the Judean people 10:22.

—2. the pl. τὰ ἔϑνη freq. is used of people outside Israel’s traditions non-Israelite persons, gentiles

—a. of non-Israelites in general and practically = polytheists Mt 6:32; Lk 12:30; Ac 14:5; Ro 3:29.

—b. of non-Israelite members of Christian congregations Ro 16:4; Gal 2:12; Eph 3:1.
